2606.30347.title: FFAvatar: Feed-Forward 4D Head Avatar Reconstruction from Sparse Portrait Images
2606.30347.paper_summary: We present FFAvatar, a Transformer-based 3D Gaussian framework for fast construction of high-quality and animatable 4D head avatars from one or more reference portrait images. Unlike existing feed-forward approaches that require a fixed number of input views, FFAvatar supports incremental reconstruction, progressively refining the avatar representation as additional reference images become available. At the core of our method is an alternating attention mechanism that disentangles identity appearance from expression and viewpoint variations, enabling the reconstruction of a canonical 3D appearance that remains consistent across poses and facial expressions. To balance visual fidelity and computational efficiency, we introduce a sparse-to-dense learning paradigm. Coarse appearance features are first learned using sparse primitives anchored to the FLAME vertex level and are subsequently densified in the UV domain to capture fine-grained geometric and texture details. We further propose a plug-and-play motion refinement module that enables subject-specific dynamic personalization by modeling residual motion beyond parametric deformation. Extensive experiments demonstrate that FFAvatar efficiently produces high-fidelity and controllable 4D head avatars, achieving superior flexibility, driving efficiency, and identity-consistent rendering across diverse expressions and viewpoints.
2606.30347.paper_summary_zh: 我們提出了FFAvatar，一種基於Transformer的3D高斯框架，旨在快速構建高品質且可動畫的4D頭像，僅需一張或多張參考肖像圖像。與現有的前饋方法需要固定數量的輸入視圖不同，FFAvatar支持增量重建，隨著額外參考圖像的可用，逐步完善頭像表示。我們方法的核心是一種交替注意機制，將身份外觀與表情和視點變化解耦，從而能夠重建在各種姿勢和面部表情中保持一致的標準3D外觀。為了平衡視覺真實性和計算效率，我們引入了一種稀疏到密集的學習範式。首先使用固定在FLAME頂點級別的稀疏原始元素學習粗略外觀特徵，然後在UV域中進行密集化，以捕捉細緻的幾何和紋理細節。我們進一步提出了一個即插即用的運動精煉模塊，通過建模超出參數變形的殘餘運動，實現針對特定主題的動態個性化。大量實驗表明，FFAvatar能有效生成高保真且可控的4D頭像，實現卓越的靈活性、驅動效率以及在多樣表情和視點中的身份一致渲染。
2606.30347.author: Jianjiang Yao et.al.
2606.30347.authors: Jianjiang Yao, Ke Xian, Renxiang Dai, Robert Caiming Qiu
